description: Peirce's classic example of the implication 'If a man, then mortal' represented as a double-cut with the antecedent outside the inner cut and the consequent inside the inner cut.
natural_language: If something is a man, then it is mortal.
test_rationale: This is one of Peirce's foundational examples that demonstrates the double-cut structure for implication. It tests the proper nesting of cuts and the correct logical interpretation of the EG structure.
